name: Poke
description: Consumer AI agent living entirely in messaging — iMessage/SMS, Telegram, WhatsApp — with no app to install. Routes each task to the best-fit model across providers; handles calendar, email, smart home, health, and purchases via recipes spanning 40+ integrations. Publicly launched March 2026; became the first third-party AI agent approved on Apple's Messages for Business (2026-06-04). Standing email/calendar access through a channel with no OS permission model is a novel risk surface.

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
evidence.value: Handles daily planning, calendar management, health tracking, smart home control, and photo edits over text; conversational disambiguation compensates for the low-bandwidth channel

task_completion_accuracy.methodology: Assessment of task completion across the recipe catalog from launch coverage and user reports; no public benchmarks exist

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
evidence.value: Recipes integrate Gmail, Google Calendar, Outlook, Notion, Linear, Strava, Oura, Fitbit, Philips Hue, Sonos, GitHub, and 40+ other services

tool_use_reliability.methodology: Review of integration breadth and reliability of server-side tool execution

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
evidence.value: Standing automations run on triggers such as every incoming email or real-time flight status, chaining multi-step actions without user prompting

multi_step_planning.methodology: Evaluation of trigger-based automations and proactive multi-step task chains

evidence.source: Poke
evidence.value: Poke is a persistent, always-on agent: one continuous conversation thread retains preferences, context, and connected-account state indefinitely

memory_persistence.methodology: Review of long-lived conversational memory, the product's core design premise

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
evidence.value: Failures surface as chat messages and the agent asks for clarification, but users cannot inspect or resume failed server-side automations

error_recovery.methodology: Assessment of failure visibility and recovery paths for background automations

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
evidence.value: Internally dispatches subtasks to the best-fit model per task across major providers and open-source options; no user-facing multi-agent composition

agent_collaboration.methodology: Review of internal model/agent routing versus user-controllable collaboration

evidence.source: Poke
evidence.value: All execution happens in Interaction's cloud rather than on the user's device; sandbox architecture and isolation between users' automations are not publicly documented

tool_sandboxing.methodology: Architecture review; cloud-side execution keeps the device safe but internal isolation is undisclosed

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform
evidence.value: Apple's Messages for Business approval (2026-06-04) adds verified-sender identity and platform review on iMessage; per-service OAuth scopes exist, but SMS/Telegram channels carry no OS-level permission model and grants are standing rather than per-task

access_control.methodology: Review of channel identity guarantees and OAuth grant model; the messaging channel bypasses OS permission prompts entirely

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
evidence.value: Automations that run on every incoming email make untrusted third-party content a standing trigger for an agent holding email, calendar, and purchase authority; no injection-hardening documentation is published

prompt_injection_defense.methodology: Threat-surface analysis: email-triggered automations are a textbook indirect prompt-injection vector with no disclosed mitigations

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
evidence.value: Company states a multi-layered security model with regular penetration testing, that it cannot access integration token contents by default, and that log/analytics sharing is opt-in; TechCrunch notes no independent audit has verified these claims

data_isolation.methodology: Review of vendor security claims; positive design statements without independent verification

evidence.source: Composio - OpenPoke: an open-source alternative to poke.com
evidence.value: Poke's agent stack is fully closed; the community reverse-engineered an open-source clone (OpenPoke) precisely because internals are unpublished

evidence.source: Poke
evidence.value: The always-on conversation model retains message history, email/calendar context, and automation state server-side indefinitely by design; consumer privacy policy offers deletion on request but no enterprise-grade retention controls

data_retention.methodology: Review of retention implications of a persistent standing agent against published policy

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
evidence.value: 10-person Palo Alto startup with no published certifications (SOC 2, ISO 27001) or DPA; WhatsApp availability in the EU was still pending regulatory/platform constraints at launch

gdpr_compliance.methodology: Compliance posture assessment for a young consumer startup; no formal attestations found

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
evidence.value: Best-fit model routing sends user content to multiple third-party model providers per task; messaging delivery relies on Linq's platform, and carrier SMS adds another processing party

third_party_data_sharing.methodology: Data flow analysis across model providers, messaging infrastructure, and carriers

evidence.source: Poke
evidence.value: Cloud-only service reachable exclusively through third-party messaging channels; no self-hosted, on-premises, or offline option

evidence.source: Poke
evidence.value: Consumer-grade site with recipe gallery and FAQs; no technical documentation of architecture, security model, or automation limits

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
evidence.value: Actions execute server-side and are reported back as chat messages; there is no action log, diff, or review step before the agent sends emails or makes purchases

execution_traceability.methodology: Review of action visibility; conversational reporting is not an audit trail

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
evidence.value: Agent narrates what it is doing and asks before consequential steps in normal use, but model-routing choices and automation internals are opaque

decision_explainability.methodology: Assessment of conversational narration versus underlying decision opacity

evidence.source: Composio - OpenPoke: an open-source alternative to poke.com
evidence.value: Entirely closed source; no published components, model cards, or security whitepapers

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
evidence.value: Community members have built thousands of custom automations with a creator payout program ($0.10-$1.00 per recipe signup); user base reportedly 10x'ed in the months after public launch

community_activity.methodology: Community engagement analysis of recipe ecosystem and growth signals

evidence.source: 9to5Mac - Apple's Messages app on iPhone now has a third-party AI agent
evidence.value: Zero-install onboarding: text Poke like a contact in Messages, Telegram, or WhatsApp; since 2026-06-04 it appears natively in Apple Messages for Business for iMessage's ~1B users

ease_of_integration.methodology: Onboarding friction assessment; messaging-native is the lowest-friction agent interface shipped to date

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform
evidence.value: 10-person team scaling to rapid consumer growth plus per-user Apple platform billing; real-time inference costs are acknowledged as the main constraint

scalability.methodology: Assessment of team size, infrastructure signals, and stated cost constraints against growth

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
evidence.value: Free for non-real-time use; paid pricing is flexible and usage-based, with beta users negotiating roughly $10-$30/mo (~$20 typical) via Poke's conversational pricing

cost_predictability.methodology: Pricing model analysis; negotiated, usage-shaped pricing is affordable but unusually unpredictable

evidence.source: Poke
evidence.value: No dashboard, usage analytics, or audit tooling; the chat thread is the only window into what the agent has done

evidence.source: TechCrunch - Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform
evidence.value: Apple's first-ever AI-agent approval on Messages for Business validates the product, and $25M raised at a $300M valuation (Spark Capital, General Catalyst) funds it, but it remains a 10-person startup prioritizing growth over profitability

production_readiness.methodology: Vendor stability and maturity assessment: strong platform validation, very young company

research-assistant.notes: Handles quick lookups and standing news/price watches over text, but has no browsing transcript or citation depth

content-creation.notes: Drafts emails and short messages well; the texting interface is unsuited to long-form work

data-analysis.notes: No analysis environment; limited to summarizing data its integrations expose

education.notes: Conversational tutoring and reminders over text suit casual learning; no structured curriculum tools

best_for: Consumers who want a personal assistant with zero app installs, usable from any phone that can text
Everyday delegation: calendar, email triage, reminders, smart home, travel check-ins, and health tracking
Standing automations triggered by incoming email or real-time events (flights, deliveries, prices)
iPhone users who want a verified agent inside Apple Messages for Business
not_recommended_for: Anyone unwilling to grant a startup standing OAuth access to their email, calendar, and messages
Regulated or enterprise contexts requiring audit trails, DPAs, or compliance certifications
High-stakes autonomous purchases or account changes without a review step
Users needing verifiable channel security over plain SMS
strengths: Lowest-friction agent interface shipped: lives entirely in iMessage/SMS, Telegram, and WhatsApp with no app
First third-party AI agent approved on Apple Messages for Business (2026-06-04), adding verified identity on iMessage
Best-fit model routing across providers and open-source models instead of single-vendor lock-in
Persistent memory and proactive trigger-based automations across 40+ integrations (Gmail, Calendar, Notion, Hue, Oura, GitHub)
Thousands of community-built recipes with creator payouts
Affordable: free tier plus flexible negotiated pricing around $10-$30/mo
limitations: Novel risk surface: standing access to email, calendar, and purchases through a channel with no OS-level permission model or revocation UI
Email-triggered automations expose the agent to indirect prompt injection from any sender, with no published hardening
Security claims (token isolation, pen testing) are vendor statements without independent audit
No action log or review step; the chat thread is the only record of what the agent did
Closed source, no compliance certifications, consumer-grade privacy policy
10-person company: continuity, support, and incident-response capacity are unproven
Negotiated usage-based pricing makes long-term costs hard to predict

supported_models: Multi-provider best-fit routing (major frontier providers and open-source models; internals undisclosed)
metadata.architecture: Cloud-hosted persistent agent reached via messaging channels (Linq-powered delivery); server-side automations with per-service OAuth connectors
metadata.deployment_type: Cloud-only; interface via iMessage/SMS, Telegram, WhatsApp (EU/Brazil availability constrained), and Apple Messages for Business
tool_support: Productivity and dev tools (Notion, Linear, GitHub, PostHog)
metadata.first_release: Beta late 2025; public launch March 2026; Apple Messages for Business approval 2026-06-04
metadata.pricing: Free tier; flexible negotiated/usage-based paid plans (~$10-$30/mo, ~$20 typical)
metadata.company: The Interaction Company of California, Palo Alto; founders Marvin von Hagen and Felix Schlegel; $15M seed (2024) + $10M (April 2026) at $300M post-money; backers include Spark Capital, General Catalyst, the Collison brothers, Guillermo Rauch
